title: Unknown Input, Zod 4 Validation, Domain Types, and Output Contracts · Contract Lens: Unknown Before Validation
summary: The service casts req.body, accepts unknown keys, transforms before authorization, returns internal objects directly, and treats TypeScript inference as runtime proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts unknown before validation increment in runtime schema and trusted domain boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model body, params, query, fields, configuration, token claims, database rows, and upstream data as unknown until runtime validation succeeds.
Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Unknown Before Validation · Theory Unknown Before Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3117 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit unknown input through strict runtime and output schemas; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model body, params, query, fields, configuration, token claims, database rows, and upstream data as unknown until runtime validation succeeds.
